use std::cell::UnsafeCell;

struct MyCell<T> {
    value: UnsafeCell<T>,
}

impl<T: Copy> MyCell<T> {
    fn get(&self) -> T {
        unsafe { *self.value.get() }
    }

    fn set(&self, val: T) {
        unsafe { *self.value.get() = val; }
    }
}

fn aliasing_violation() {
    let cell = MyCell { value: UnsafeCell::new(42i32) };
    let ref1 = &cell;
    let ptr = ref1.value.get();
    let ref2 = unsafe { &*ptr };
    let val = cell.get();
    println!("{} {}", ref2, val);
}

unsafe fn mutate_through_alias(data: &mut [u8], idx1: usize, idx2: usize) {
    let ptr = data.as_mut_ptr();
    let a = &mut *ptr.add(idx1);
    let b = &mut *ptr.add(idx2);
    *a = 1;
    *b = 2;
}
